title: melicope obtusifolia subsp. obtusifolia
intro: Melicope obtusifolia subsp. obtusifolia, also known as the Hawaiian lemon myrtle or lemon myrtle, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It is a shrub or small tree that has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine, valued for its antimicrobial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's leaves and bark have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, sore throats, and digestive issues, and have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. The leaves can be prepared into teas, infusions, or essential oils, which are used in traditional Hawaiian medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
